page: Amazon Web Services c8g.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. Utilizing the arm64 architecture, this instance provides 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, with one thread per core. The memory configuration consists of 96.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The processor features 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but it features a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance and exceptional memory bandwidth. The instance achieves top-tier ratings in large-block memory read and write operations, reaching 395.06 GB/sec and 184.52 GB/sec respectively, alongside a top-tier PassMark memory score. Multi-threaded operations scale efficiently, as evidenced by top-tier results in Geekbench multi-core tests, software compilation with Clang, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Single-core performance is rated as strong to average across Geekbench and stress-ng tests.

Given its compute-focused resource density and high memory bandwidth, the c8g.12xlarge is highly efficient for workloads requiring parallel processing without massive memory footprints. It demonstrates top-tier performance in database operations and static web serving throughput. The instance is well-suited for software compilation, multi-threaded data decompression, web serving, and database workloads. The absence of local storage and GPUs represents a tradeoff, making it less suitable for local disk-heavy storage tasks or hardware-accelerated machine learning training, though it maintains average performance for small to medium LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8g.12xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance powered by 48 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 96.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 2.0 GB of memory per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier capabilities in multi-core processing, software compilation, and large-block memory bandwidth, alongside strong database operations and static web serving throughput. Single-core performance remains average to strong. This profile makes the c8g.12xlarge highly efficient for parallelized compute workloads, software development pipelines, web serving, and database applications that do not require local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.12xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 96 GB of RAM, and 22 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.12xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 48 dedicated Graviton cores, 96 GB RAM, and 22 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized platform featuring forty-eight dedicated cores and high-speed memory bandwidth for parallel processing and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 arm64 cores running at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core
Delivers top-tier multi-core performance, software compilation, and memory bandwidth
Features 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU accelerators
Optimized for multi-threaded applications, database operations, and static web serving
